J1.rationale: Explicitly identifies the pivot (live=Lumina meeting assistant, layout metadata + GSC=legacy InboxZap), states the trust/bounce implication, and frames every recommendation around the new positioning while explicitly refusing to build on inbox-cleanup demand.

J2.basis: `inboxzap` — 320 impressions, 14 clicks, avg position 4.1 ... legacy `/blog/post-1..12` carrying 600–3,900 impressions each, positions 42–58, 301'd to /; 3 harvest / 37 sunset with per-query intent reasoning
J2.rationale: Assigns real dispositions (harvest branded via FAQ, 301 legacy posts, sunset 37) grounded in cited GSC impressions/positions; the 37 sunset queries are handled as a data-justified tier (pos 38–65, 0–1 clicks) rather than truly per-asset, and 301s route to `/` rather than topical successors, keeping it just short of the top anchor.

J3.basis: overrode [the auto-classifier] — Lumina has no email feature, so that traffic can never convert... branded searches still convert at position 4–9 — real equity, now captured via a homepage FAQ item bridging old brand to new; 301s so the equity isn't hard-dropped
J3.rationale: Names the tension explicitly and lands a defensible middle path: rejects blindly harvesting dead email demand (invented-demand reasoning) while preserving branded equity via FAQ + soft 301s and building the meeting-assistant cluster as the growth engine with a sequenced transition.
